enrichment.summary: Kyle gives his opinion on both Trump impeachments, arguing both were justified. He explains the Ukraine/Burisma quid pro quo underlying the first impeachment, and argues the Capitol riot constitutes clear incitement warranting the second. He also opens by announcing he has quit marijuana and is returning to making videos.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Valid and should have resulted in conviction — the Ukraine extortion violated the Emoluments Clause and common sense
Obviously justified — Trump incited the Capitol riot through repeated coded language and delayed calling the National Guard
Legitimate and necessary to bar Trump from running again, not mere vengeance
Republicans rely on straw man arguments instead of substantive defense
Republicans exist to serve the wealthy by stoking culture-war passions among low-information voters
Trump is a demagogue unlike any prior president; law should require prior public office before running for president
U.S. has been dangerously soft on China, especially on military technology and manufacturing
Desires elected office because he believes he would engage honestly rather than deflect

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is a request video from about a year ago about the impeachment, and now they're having another impeachment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This is his first video back because he doesn't have reefers anymore.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He huffed reefer for the last time yesterday, huffing a big old nugget for about an hour because he knew it was going to be the last time he was ever going to smoke reefer in his entire life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to be a political figure, like a Congressman or president, because we need more politicians who think like he does.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When talking about a legal issue, you should always discuss the history, precedent, what the law says, and what's best for the future.

claims.subject: Rome
claims.claim: Rome is where all the history is, and you would talk about Rome when discussing the first time impeachment ever happened.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Donald Trump was impeached the first time because he tried to blackmail the president of Ukraine into producing a news headline about investigating Joe Biden's son, Hunter Biden, for taking money from Burisma.

claims.subject: Hunter Biden
claims.claim: Hunter Biden took $500,000 from Burisma, a natural gas company in Ukraine.

claims.subject: right-wing politicians
claims.claim: Right-wing politicians use straw man arguments, taking them way too far, like Marjorie Taylor Greene and Kellyanne Conway.

claims.subject: Marjorie Taylor Greene
claims.claim: Marjorie Taylor Greene was interviewed about supporting the insurrection and thinking the election was rigged, but instead of answering, she turned it on the person asking the question.

claims.subject: Kellyanne Conway
claims.claim: Kellyanne Conway turns questions on the person asking them, avoiding the actual topic.

claims.subject: Jim Jordan
claims.claim: Jim Jordan has a whiny voice and straw mans arguments during impeachment hearings.

claims.subject: Devin Nunes
claims.claim: Devin Nunes was making money off the Ukrainian natural gas deal and was involved in knocking down Hunter and Joe Biden.

claims.subject: Stephanie (unnamed)
claims.claim: A young woman named Stephanie, about 33, was super nasty and accused people of mistreating her on the Senate floor.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Trump said he wouldn't give Ukraine $40 million for Tomahawk missiles unless they agreed to do a story about Hunter Biden first.

claims.subject: Emoluments Clause
claims.claim: The Emoluments Clause of the Constitution prohibits asking for personal favors in official dealings, and people have been busted for violating it.

claims.subject: Bill Clinton
claims.claim: Bill Clinton was impeached for lying about a personal sex thing but wasn't convicted.

claims.subject: Richard Nixon
claims.claim: Nixon was impeached for paying people to break into his opponent's hotel room at Watergate, but he resigned before being convicted.

claims.subject: Al Franken
claims.claim: Al Franken resigned because a lady said he told her she had nice tits.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: The first impeachment of Trump was valid because he violated common sense and the law, and the opposition had no sensible reasons.

claims.subject: two-party system
claims.claim: The two-party system exists because the right-wing is full of idiots, and the left-wing is where common sense resides.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Trump incited a riot at the Capitol, and he should be impeached for it.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Trump told his followers they were badasses and that it would be wild, which led to the Capitol riot.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Trump didn't call in the National Guard for two hours during the Capitol riot and tweeted that the election was unfairly stolen.

claims.subject: Rudy Giuliani
claims.claim: Rudy Giuliani said 'trial by combat' during the Capitol riot.

claims.subject: Don Jr.
claims.claim: Don Jr. said they would go after bad Republicans and hoped Mike Pence would do the right thing, or they would have to show them.

claims.subject: post-presidential impeachment
claims.claim: A post-presidential impeachment is to prevent Trump from running again in 2024.

claims.subject: Mitt Romney
claims.claim: Mitt Romney said Trump would be the nominee in 2024.

claims.subject: Donald Trump
claims.claim: Trump is a demagogue who brainwashes people with uncontrolled rage.

claims.subject: China
claims.claim: China has taken over manufacturing of high-end missile magnets, and the U.S. no longer knows how to make them.

claims.subject: David K. Johnston
claims.claim: David K. Johnston wrote about how the U.S. outsourced missile magnet designs to China.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He will do request videos now that he's sober and needs to think of stuff to talk about.
